Add or import a WireGuard profile, then: Add or import a WireGuard profile, then choose a SOCKS5 host and port for that profile.
Closing the window hides it when tray su: Closing the window hides it when tray support is available. Use Quit from the tray menu to stop all profiles, wait briefly for them to close, and exit.
Connect starts the embedded WireGuard en: Connect starts the embedded WireGuard engine and SOCKS5 listener for the selected profile. The app does not launch the wireproxy command-line tool. Connect All starts every saved profile. Runtime status is shown in the selected profile log.
Disconnect a connected profile, and wait: Disconnect a connected profile, and wait for it to finish disconnecting, before changing its WireGuard configuration or SOCKS5 bind address. Profile name and startup preference can be changed while connected.
Each connected, connecting, or disconnec: Each connected, connecting, or disconnecting profile must use a unique SOCKS5 bind address, such as 127.0.0.1:1080 and 127.0.0.1:1081.
Import and Export open the operating sys: Import and Export open the operating system's native file dialog and use the selected filesystem path. Export writes profiles as JSON. Import accepts exported JSON bundles or WireGuard .conf files.
Name: Name
SOCKS5 port must be a number: SOCKS5 port must be a number
SOCKS5 port must be between 1 and 65535: SOCKS5 port must be between 1 and 65535
The profile log follows the newest line: The profile log follows the newest line by default. Scrolling up pauses following so you can read earlier output; scrolling back to the bottom follows new lines again.
The tray menu has one profile row per pr: The tray menu has one profile row per profile. The colored dot shows connection status, and each submenu shows status text, SOCKS5 bind address, WireGuard IP, and Connect or Disconnect actions. The icon is green only while connected; other states use a red icon and the status text shows disconnected, connecting, disconnecting, or error.
